info.title: Zendesk Support API (curated subset)
info.description: Curated subset of the real, published Atlassian spec (source: zendesk.json), trimmed to the operations Anvil backtests against a mature reference MCP server.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Lists tickets by external id. External ids don't have to be unique for each ticket. As a result, the request may return multiple tickets with the same external id.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description:
Unix epoch time to filter tickets. Only tickets created or updated after
this time are returned. Example: `?start_time=1332034771`

tickets.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

Location.description: The URL of the created ticket

ticket.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

get.description:
Returns a number of ticket properties, but doesn't include the full comment thread. The initial comment is available in the ticket's [description property](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#json-format). To retrieve all the ticket's comments, use [List Comments](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket_comments/#list-comments).

#### Allowed For
* Agents

parameters.name: reduced_payload_size
parameters.description: When true, returns a reduced ticket payload (omits null custom fields).

parameters.name: remove_duplicate_fields
parameters.description: When true, removes duplicate custom field entries from the response.

ticket.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

comment.body: Thanks for choosing Acme Jet Motors.

delete.description:
#### Allowed For

* Admins
* Agents with permission to delete tickets

Agent delete permissions are set in Support. See
[Deleting tickets](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408883872538)
in the Support Help Center.

#### Ticket deletion rate limit

You can delete 400 tickets every 1 minute using this endpoint.
The rate limiting mechanism behaves as described in
[Rate limits](/api-reference/introduction/rate-limits/) in the API introduction.
Zendesk recommends that you obey the Retry-After header values.
To delete many tickets, you may use [Bulk Delete Tickets](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#bulk-delete-tickets).

get.description:
Returns the comments added to the ticket.

Each comment may include a `content_url` for an attachment or a `recording_url` for a voice comment that points to a file that may be hosted externally. For security reasons, take care not to inadvertently send Zendesk authentication credentials to third parties when attempting to access these files. See [Working with url properties](/documentation/api-basics/best-practices/working-with-url-properties/).

#### Pagination

- Cursor pagination (recommended)
- Offset pagination

See [Pagination](/api-reference/introduction/pagination/).

Returns a maximum of 100 records per page.

#### Sorting

By default, comments are sorted by creation date in ascending order.

When using cursor pagination, use the following parameter to change the sort order:

| Name   | Type   | Required | Comments
| ------ | ------ | -------- | --------
| `sort` | string | no       | Possible values are "created_at" (ascending order) or "-created_at" (descending order)

When using offset pagination, use the following parameters to change the sort order:

| Name         | Type   | Required | Comments
| ------------ | ------ | -------- | --------
| `sort_order` | string | no       | One of `asc`, `desc`. Defaults to `asc`

#### Allowed For

* Agents

parameters.name: include_inline_images
parameters.description: Default is false. When true, inline images are also listed as attachments in the response

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Accepts "users". Use this parameter to list email CCs by side-loading users. Example: `?include=users`. **Note**: If the comment source is email, a deleted user will be represented as the CCd email address. If the comment source is anything else, a deleted user will be represented as the user name.

get.description:
#### Pagination

* Cursor pagination (recommended)
* Offset pagination

See [Pagination](/api-reference/introduction/pagination/).

Returns a maximum of 100 records per page.

#### Allowed For

* Admins, Agents and Light Agents

parameters.name: brand_id
parameters.description:
When brand separation is enabled, scopes the listing to users belonging to
the specified brand. Only applicable when the account has brand separation enabled.

Accepted values:

* "all" — return all users across all brands (no brand filtering applied).
* 0 — return only account-scoped (brand-less) users.
* A numeric brand id — if the brand has user separation enabled, return
  end users belonging to that brand plus all agents and admins (who are
  account-level and always included). If the brand does not have user
  separation enabled, the request falls back to account scope (0).

TicketSupportTypeScope.name: support_type_scope
TicketSupportTypeScope.description: Lists tickets by support type. Possible values are "all", "agent", or "ai_agent". Defaults to "agent"

DualPaginationPage.name: page
DualPaginationPage.description:
Pagination parameter. Supports both traditional offset and cursor-based pagination:

- Traditional: `?page=2` (integer page number)
- Cursor: `?page[size]=50&page[after]=cursor` (deepObject with size, after, before)

These are mutually exclusive - use one format or the other, not both.

oneOf.description: Traditional offset pagination page number (non-negative integer)

PerPage.name: per_page
PerPage.description:
Number of records to return per page.

Note: Default and maximum values vary by endpoint. Check endpoint-specific
documentation for limits.

CursorPaginationSort.name: sort
CursorPaginationSort.description:
Field to sort results by. Prefix with `-` for descending order.

When used with cursor pagination, this determines the cursor ordering.

Example: `?sort=name` or `?sort=-created_at`

TicketsInclude.name: include
TicketsInclude.description:
Sideloads to include in the response. Accepts a comma-separated list of values.
See [Sideloading](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#sideloading).

CursorPaginationPage.name: page
CursorPaginationPage.description:
Cursor-based pagination parameters (JSON:API style).

Supports nested parameters:
- `page[size]` - Number of records per page (default varies by endpoint, typically 100)
- `page[after]` - Cursor token to fetch records after this position
- `page[before]` - Cursor token to fetch records before this position

Example: `?page[size]=50&page[after]=eyJvIjoiaWQiLCJ2IjoiYVFFPSJ9`

after.description: Cursor token for fetching next page

before.description: Cursor token for fetching previous page

UserRoleFilter.name: role
UserRoleFilter.description: Filters the results by role. Possible values are "end-user", "agent", "admin", or a custom role name

UserRolesFilter.name: role[]
UserRolesFilter.description: Filters the results by more than one role using the format `role[]={role}&role[]={role}`

UserPermissionSetFilter.name: permission_set
UserPermissionSetFilter.description: For custom roles which is available on the Enterprise plan and above. You can only filter by one role ID per request

UserExternalIdFilter.name: external_id
UserExternalIdFilter.description: List users by external id. External id has to be unique for each user under the same account.

UsersInclude.name: include
UsersInclude.description:
Sideloads to include in the response. Accepts a comma-separated list of values.
See [Sideloading](/api-reference/ticketing/users/users/#sideloading).

IncludeBoundaryIndicators.name: include_boundary_indicators
IncludeBoundaryIndicators.description:
When true, includes `has_more` indicator in the cursor pagination response meta.

Only valid with cursor pagination (page[size], page[after], page[before]).

IncludeItemCursors.name: include_item_cursors
IncludeItemCursors.description:
When true, includes cursor values for each item in the cursor pagination response.

Only valid with cursor pagination (page[size], page[after], page[before]).

system_metadata.description: System metadata for the request, typically set by internal clients
System metadata for the request, typically set by internal clients

additional_collaborators.description: Write only. An array of numeric IDs, emails, or objects containing name and email properties. See [Setting Collaborators](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#setting-collaborators). An email notification is sent to them when the ticket is updated

allow_attachments.description: Permission for agents to add add attachments to a comment. Defaults to true

allow_channelback.description: Is false if channelback is disabled, true otherwise. Only applicable for channels framework ticket

assignee_email.description: Write only. The email address of the agent to assign the ticket to

assignee_id.description: The agent currently assigned to the ticket

attribute_value_ids.description: Write only. An array of the IDs of attribute values to be associated with the ticket

brand_id.description: The id of the brand this ticket is associated with. See [Setting up multiple brands](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408829476378)

collaborator_ids.description: The ids of users currently CC'ed on the ticket

collaborators.description: Write only. Users to add as cc's when creating a ticket. See [Setting Collaborators](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets#setting-collaborators)

custom_fields.description:
Custom field values to set on the ticket.
Each entry specifies a field ID and its value.
See [Setting custom field values](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets#setting-custom-field-values)

custom_status_id.description: The custom ticket status id of the ticket. See [custom ticket statuses](#custom-ticket-statuses)

description.description:
The first comment on the ticket. When [creating a ticket](#create-ticket), use `comment` to set the description.
See [Description and first comment](#description-and-first-comment).
On create, can be set directly as an alternative to `comment`. Silently ignored on update.

due_at.description: If this is a ticket of type "task" it has a due date.  Due date format uses [ISO 8601](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format

email_cc_ids.description: The ids of agents or end users currently CC'ed on the ticket. See [CCs and followers resources](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408822451482) in the Support Help Center

email_ccs.description: Write only. An array of objects that represent agent or end users email CCs to add or delete from the ticket. See [Setting email CCs](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets/#setting-email-ccs). Ignored when [CCs and followers](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408822451482) is not enabled

encoded_id.description: An encoded string representing the ticket's unique identifier

external_id.description: An id you can use to link Zendesk Support tickets to local records

fields.description:
Alias for custom_fields. Custom field values to set on the ticket.
Each entry specifies a field ID and its value.

follower_ids.description: The ids of agents currently following the ticket. Ignored when [CCs and followers](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408822451482) is not enabled

followers.description: Write only. An array of objects that represents agent followers to add or delete from the ticket. See [Setting followers](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets/#setting-followers). Ignored when [CCs and followers](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408822451482) is not enabled

followup_ids.description: The ids of the followups created from this ticket. Ids are only visible once the ticket is closed

forum_topic_id.description: The topic in the Zendesk Web portal this ticket originated from, if any. The Web portal is deprecated

from_messaging_channel.description: If true, the ticket's [via type](/documentation/ticketing/reference-guides/via-object-reference/) is a messaging channel.

generated_timestamp.description: A Unix timestamp that represents the most accurate reading of when this record was last updated. It is updated for all ticket updates, including system updates

group_id.description: The group this ticket is assigned to

has_incidents.description: Is true if a ticket is a problem type and has one or more incidents linked to it. Otherwise, the value is false.

id.description: Automatically assigned when the ticket is created

is_public.description: Is true if any comments are public, false otherwise

macro_id.description: Write only. A single macro ID to be recorded in the ticket audit

macro_ids.description: Write only. List of macro IDs to be recorded in the ticket audit

metadata.description: Write only. Metadata for the audit. In the `audit` object, the data is specified in the `custom` property of the `metadata` object. See [Setting Metadata](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets/#setting-metadata)

organization_id.description: The organization of the requester. You can only specify the ID of an organization associated with the requester. See [Organization Memberships](/api-reference/ticketing/organizations/organization_memberships/)

origin_zrn.description: Write only. The Zendesk Resource Name (ZRN) of the origin for this ticket

priority.description: The urgency with which the ticket should be addressed

problem_id.description: For tickets of type "incident", the ID of the problem the incident is linked to

raw_subject.description:
The dynamic content placeholder, if present, or the "subject" value, if not. See [Dynamic Content Items](/api-reference/ticketing/ticket-management/dynamic_content/).
When set on create or update, overrides the `subject` field.

recipient.description: The original recipient e-mail address of the ticket. Notification emails for the ticket are sent from this address

requester.description:
Write only. Alternative to requester_id for specifying the ticket requester.

Can be provided as:
- Email string: "user@example.com"
- User ID integer: 12345
- User object: {name: "John Doe", email: "john@example.com"}

If both requester and requester_id are provided, requester_id takes precedence.

When creating a ticket, if the user doesn't exist, they may be created
automatically depending on account settings.

organization_id.description: The organization ID to associate with the user

verified.description: Whether the user's email is verified

requester_id.description: The user who requested this ticket

safe_update.description: Write only. Optional boolean. When true and an `updated_stamp` date is included, protects against ticket update collisions and returns a message to let you know if one occurs. See [Protecting against ticket update collisions](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets/#protecting-against-ticket-update-collisions). A value of false has the same effect as true. Omit the property to force the updates to not be safe

satisfaction_rating.description: The satisfaction rating of the ticket, if it exists, or the state of satisfaction, "offered" or "unoffered". The value is null for plan types that don't support CSAT

sharing_agreement_ids.description: An array of the numeric IDs of sharing agreements. Note that this replaces any existing agreements

sharing_agreements.description:
Write only. Sharing agreement configuration for the ticket.
Accepts a single object or an array of objects.

status.description:
The state of the ticket.

If your account has activated custom ticket statuses, this is the ticket's
status category. See [custom ticket statuses](#custom-ticket-statuses)

subject.description: The value of the subject field for this ticket. See [Subject](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#subject)

submitter_id.description:
The user who submitted the ticket. The submitter always becomes the
author of the first comment on the ticket.

If not specified, defaults to the authenticated user.

support_type.description: The type of support that handled the ticket

suspended_ticket_id.description: Write only. The ID of the suspended ticket being recovered

suspension_type_id.description: Write only. The suspension type ID when recovering a suspended ticket

system_metadata.description: Write only. System metadata for the ticket, typically set by internal clients

tags.description: The tags applied to this ticket. Unless otherwise specified, the [set tag](/api-reference/ticketing/ticket-management/tags/#set-tags) behavior is used, which overwrites and replaces existing tags

tde_workspace.description: Write only. TDE workspace information for the ticket

ticket_form_id.description: Enterprise only. The id of the ticket form to render for the ticket

tpe_voice_comment.description: Write only. A TPE voice comment for Talk Partner Edition tickets

updated_at.description: When this record last got updated. It is updated only if the update generates a [ticket event](#incremental-ticket-event-export)

updated_stamp.description: Write only. Datetime of last update received from API. See the `safe_update` property

url.description: The API url of this ticket

via_followup_source_id.description: Write only. The id of a closed ticket when creating a follow-up ticket. See [Creating a follow-up ticket](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/creating-and-updating-tickets#creating-a-follow-up-ticket)

via_id.description: Write only. For more information, see the [Via object reference](/documentation/ticketing/reference-guides/via-object-reference/)

voice_comment.description: Write only. A voice comment for Talk tickets. See [Adding voice comments to tickets](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/adding-voice-comments-to-tickets/)

answered_by_id.description: The agent who answered the call

call_duration.description: Duration of the call in seconds

example.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

add_short_url.description: Internal flag for adding short URLs to the comment

audit_id.description: The id of the ticket audit record. See [Show Audit](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket_audits/#show-audit)

author_id.description: The id of the comment author. If null or omitted on create, defaults to the authenticated user. See [Author id](#author-id)

channel_back.description: Internal channel back identifier for the comment

channel_source_id.description: Internal channel source identifier for the comment

created_at.description: The time the comment was created

html_body.description: The comment formatted as HTML. See [Bodies](#bodies)

id.description: Automatically assigned when the comment is created

metadata.description: System information (web client, IP address, etc.) and comment flags, if any. See [Comment flags](#comment-flags)

plain_body.description: The comment presented as plain text. See [Bodies](#bodies)

public.description: true if a public comment; false if an internal note. The initial value set on ticket creation persists for any additional comment unless you change it

translate_to.description: The locale code to translate the comment body to.

type.description: `Comment` or `VoiceComment`. The JSON object for adding voice comments to tickets is different. See [Adding voice comments to tickets](/documentation/ticketing/managing-tickets/adding-voice-comments-to-tickets)

uploads.description: List of tokens received from [uploading files](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket-attachments/#upload-files) for comment attachments. The files are attached by creating or updating tickets with the tokens. See [Attaching files](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/tickets/#attaching-files) in Tickets

system.client: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_12_6) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/61.0.3163.100 Safari/537.36

TicketFieldValueInput.description: A ticket custom field value assignment

id.description: The ID of the custom field

value.description:
The value to assign to the custom field.
Type depends on the field configuration:
- Text/textarea: string
- Checkbox: boolean or "true"/"false" string
- Dropdown/tagger: string or array of strings (multi-select)
- Number/decimal: number or string representation
- Date: string (ISO 8601 format)

SharingAgreementInput.description: A sharing agreement configuration to apply to a ticket

id.description: The ID of the sharing agreement

ViaObject.description: An object explaining how the ticket was created. See the [Via object reference](/documentation/ticketing/reference-guides/via-object-reference)

channel.description:
This tells you how the ticket or event was created. Examples: "web", "mobile", "rule", "system".
May be a string name or an integer channel ID.

source.description: For some channels a source object gives more information about how or why the ticket or event was created

AttachmentObject.description: A file represented as an [Attachment](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket-attachments/) object

TicketAuditViaObject.description: Describes how the object was created. See the [Via object reference](/documentation/ticketing/reference-guides/via-object-reference)

channel.description: This tells you how the ticket or event was created. Examples: "web", "mobile", "rule", "system"

source.description: For some channels a source object gives more information about how or why the ticket or event was created

active.description: false if the user has been deleted

agent_brand_ids.description: PUT or POST requests only. Assigns agent or agents to a brand. For more information, see [Agent brand ids](#agent-brand-ids)

alias.description: An alias displayed to end users

chat_only.description: Whether or not the user is a chat-only agent

created_at.description: The time the user was created

custom_role_id.description: A custom role if the user is an agent on the Enterprise plan or above

default_group_id.description: The id of the user's default group

details.description: Any details you want to store about the user, such as an address

email.description: The user's primary email address. *Writeable on create only. On update, a secondary email is added. See [Email Address](#email-address)

external_id.description: A unique identifier from another system. The API treats the id as case insensitive. Example: "ian1" and "IAN1" are the same value.

iana_time_zone.description: The time zone for the user

id.description: Automatically assigned when the user is created

last_login_at.description:
Last time the user signed in to Zendesk Support or made an API request
using an API token

locale.description: The user's locale. A BCP-47 compliant tag for the locale. If both "locale" and "locale_id" are present on create or update, "locale_id" is ignored and only "locale" is used.

moderator.description: Designates whether the user has forum moderation capabilities

notes.description: Any notes you want to store about the user

only_private_comments.description: true if the user can only create private comments

organization_id.description: The id of the user's organization. If the user has more than one [organization memberships](/api-reference/ticketing/organizations/organization_memberships/), the id of the user's default organization. If updating, see [Organization ID](#organization-id)

phone.description: The user's primary phone number. See [Phone Number](#phone-number) below

photo.description: The user's profile picture represented as an [Attachment](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket-attachments/) object

remote_photo_url.description: A URL pointing to the user's profile picture.

report_csv.description:
This parameter is inert and has no effect. It may be deprecated in the
future.

Previously, this parameter determined whether a user could access a CSV
report in a legacy Guide dashboard. This dashboard has been removed. See
[Announcing Guide legacy reporting upgrade to
Explore](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4762263171610-Announcing-Guide-legacy-reporting-upgrade-to-Explore-)

restricted_agent.description: If the agent has any restrictions; false for admins and unrestricted agents, true for other agents

role.description: The user's role. Possible values are "end-user", "agent", or "admin"

role_type.description: The user's role id. 0 for a custom agent, 1 for a light agent, 2 for a chat agent, 3 for a chat agent added to the Support account as a contributor ([Chat Phase 4](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408836197658#topic_p1h_sx4_pgb)), 4 for an admin, and 5 for a billing admin

separation.description:
The brand separation scope for the user. Only present when the account has brand separation enabled.
For end users, indicates whether they are scoped to the full account or a specific brand.
Staff users (agents and admins) always have `scope: "account"`.

brand_id.description: The id of the brand the user is scoped to. Only present when "scope" is "brand".

scope.description: The separation scope. "account" means account-level access; "brand" means access limited to a specific brand.

shared.description: If the user is shared from a different Zendesk Support instance. Shared users can be added to organizations but cannot be modified through update requests. Any attempt to update a shared user results in a 403 Forbidden error. Ticket sharing accounts only

shared_agent.description: If the user is a shared agent from a different Zendesk Support instance. Ticket sharing accounts only

shared_phone_number.description: Whether the `phone` number is shared or not. See [Phone Number](#phone-number) below

signature.description: The user's signature. Only agents and admins can have signatures

suspended.description: If the agent is suspended. Tickets from suspended users are also suspended, and these users cannot sign in to the end user portal

suspension_details.description: Channel-level suspension state for the user. The value is null if the user has no active channel-level suspension

channels.description: Channels on which the user is suspended. `["all"]` means suspended across every channel. If "all" is present, it must be the only element

tags.description: The user's tags. Only present if your account has user tagging enabled

ticket_restriction.description: Specifies which tickets the user has access to. Possible values are: "organization", "groups", "assigned", "requested", null. "groups" and "assigned" are valid only for agents. If you pass an invalid value to an end user (for example, "groups"), they will be assigned to "requested", regardless of their previous access

time_zone.description: The user's time zone. See [Time Zone](#time-zone)

two_factor_auth_enabled.description: If two factor authentication is enabled

updated_at.description: The time the user was last updated

user_fields.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile. See [User Fields](#user-fields)

verified.description: Any of the user's identities is verified. See [User Identities](/api-reference/ticketing/users/user_identities)

created_at.description: The time the user was created

email.description: The primary email address of this user. If the primary email address is not [verified](https://support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408886752410), the secondary email address is used

iana_time_zone.description: The time zone for the user

locale_id.description: The language identifier for this user

organization_id.description: The id of the user's organization. If the user has more than one [organization memberships](/api-reference/ticketing/organizations/organization_memberships/), the id of the user's default organization. If updating, see [Organization ID](/api-reference/ticketing/users/users/#organization-id)

phone.description: The primary phone number of this user. See [Phone Number](/api-reference/ticketing/users/users/#phone-number) in the Users API

photo.description: The user's profile picture represented as an [Attachment](/api-reference/ticketing/tickets/ticket-attachments/) object

role.description: The role of the user. Possible values: `"end-user"`, `"agent"`, `"admin"`

shared_phone_number.description: Whether the `phone` number is shared or not. See [Phone Number](/api-reference/ticketing/users/users/#phone-number) in the Users API

updated_at.description: The time of the last update of the user

url.description: The API url of this user

verified.description: Any of the user's identities is verified. See [User Identities](/api-reference/ticketing/users/user_identities)

active_brand_id.description: The id of the brand to assign to the user

alias.description: An alias displayed to end users

custom_role_id.description: A custom role if the user is an agent on the Enterprise plan or above

default_group_id.description: The id of the user's default group

details.description: Any details you want to store about the user, such as an address

external_id.description: A unique identifier from another system

group_id.description: The id of the user's default group. Alias for default_group_id

group_ids.description: The ids of the groups to assign to the user

language.description: The user's language preference. Alias for locale.

locale.description: The user's locale. A BCP-47 compliant tag for the locale. If both "locale" and "locale_id" are present on create or update, "locale_id" is ignored and only "locale" is used.

moderator.description: Designates whether the user has forum moderation capabilities

notes.description: Any notes you want to store about the user

only_private_comments.description: true if the user can only create private comments

organization_id.description: The id of the user's organization

organization_ids.description: The ids of the user's organizations

organization_name.description: The name of the user's organization (deprecated, use organization object instead)

remote_photo_url.description: A URL pointing to the user's profile picture

separation.description:
Assigns the end user to a specific brand when brand separation is enabled.
Only applicable to end users (not agents or admins). Immutable after creation.

oneOf.title: AccountScope
oneOf.description: Scopes the user to the full account.

oneOf.title: BrandScope
oneOf.description: Scopes the user to a specific brand.

brand_id.description: The id of the brand to scope the end user to.

shared_phone_number.description: Whether the phone number is shared or not

signature.description: The user's signature. Only agents and admins can have signatures

skip_verify_email.description: If true, the user won't receive a verification email

ticket_restriction.description: Specifies which tickets the user has access to

user_fields.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile

verified.description: Any of the user's identities is verified

UserMergeInput.description:
Variant for create_or_update when identifying an existing user by id,
email, or external_id. No fields are strictly required since the caller
may use any of these identifiers.

user_fields.description: Values of custom fields in the user's profile

content_type.description: The content type of the image. Example value: "image/png"

content_url.description: A full URL where the attachment image file can be downloaded. The file may be hosted externally so take care not to inadvertently send Zendesk authentication credentials. See [Working with url properties](/documentation/api-basics/best-practices/working-with-url-properties/)

deleted.description: If true, the attachment has been deleted

file_name.description: The name of the image file

height.description: The height of the image file in pixels. If height is unknown, returns null

inline.description:
If true, the attachment is excluded from the attachment list and the attachment's URL
can be referenced within the comment of a ticket. Default is false

malware_access_override.description: If true, you can download an attachment flagged as malware. If false, you can't download such an attachment.

malware_scan_result.description: The result of the malware scan. There is a delay between the time the attachment is uploaded and when the malware scan is completed. Usually the scan is done within a few seconds, but high load conditions can delay the scan results. Possible values: "malware_found", "malware_not_found", "failed_to_scan", "not_scanned"

mapped_content_url.description: The URL the attachment image file has been mapped to

size.description: The size of the image file in bytes

url.description: A URL to access the attachment details

width.description: The width of the image file in pixels. If width is unknown, returns null

thumbnails.description: An array of attachment objects. Note that photo thumbnails do not have thumbnails

primary.description: If the identity is the primary identity. Writable only when creating, not when updating.

verification_method.description: Indicates the state of user identity verification.

verified.description: If the identity has been verified. Deprecated. Use `verification_method` as a more accurate representation of a user's state of verification.

tickets.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

ticket.subject: Help, my printer is on fire!

comment.body: Thanks for choosing Acme Jet Motors.
